description_long: This is an irregularly shaped fragment of stone, predominantly dark gray with lighter marbling and a partially preserved painted image on one face. The painted surface features a faint depiction of a human face, possibly from a mural or fresco, with delicate white and beige tones suggesting a classical or historical style. The stone's edges are jagged and broken, indicating it is a remnant of a larger piece. The reverse and side views show a rough, unadorned stone surface, lacking any visible markings or carvings. The overall texture appears coarse, with a matte finish and some surface pitting. This object could serve as a historical artifact or decorative element in an urban park or heritage site.
description: A jagged, dark gray stone fragment with a partially preserved painted human face on one side and rough, unadorned surfaces on the others.
description_view_0: The front view displays the painted human face on a marbled dark gray stone surface, with jagged edges and visible weathering.
description_view_1: The side view reveals a plain, rough stone surface with no visible markings or paint, emphasizing the stone's thickness.
description_view_2: This view again shows the painted face, similar to view 0, but from a slightly different angle, highlighting the irregular edge contours.
description_view_3: The opposite side view is plain and unmarked, showing only the stone's natural texture and color.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or displayed as a relic
attribute_pattern: Irregular marbling with a partially preserved painted human face; no regular geometric or repetitive patterns.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and matte with visible pitting and some fading of the painted area, indicating moderate weathering and age.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured or paved urban environments, drawing attention as a historical or artistic artifact; may be placed on a pedestal or within a landscaped area.
